selected_issue.issue: canonical checks fail to run on Linux automation hosts due to missing bootstrap path
selected_issue.title: Add Linux/macOS bootstrap flow so canonical test and replay evidence can be collected

why_it_outranked_alternatives: Ranked using current evidence: no runnable structural-check evidence was available before setup because runtime dependencies were missing; rank-2 failing canonical checks was reproducible on agent-base (`python3 -m pytest -q` failed with No module named pytest, and replay assessment failed with No module named fastapi).

checks.result: only scripts/bootstrap.ps1 existed; no Linux/macOS bootstrap script

files_changed.change: added Linux/macOS bootstrap with venv creation, virtualenv fallback, incomplete-venv recovery, and dev dependency install
documented Linux/macOS bootstrap command in setup section

validation_performed.result: initial run revealed ensurepip/venv availability issue; informed fallback fix
success after fallback/recovery fix; .venv311 created and requirements installed

before_after_evidence.before: Canonical commands were blocked on Linux automation host due to missing test/runtime packages and no Linux bootstrap path.
before_after_evidence.after: Linux/macOS bootstrap path exists and successfully prepares .venv311; canonical pytest and replay assessment run cleanly.

unresolved_risks: Playwright host dependency warnings may remain on minimal Linux hosts unless OS browser libraries are installed.
Run scoped to bootstrap/evidence-collection enablement; no broader infra packaging changes included.
stop_reason: Stopped after fixing the selected rank-2 canonical-check execution issue with minimal changes and repeated validation; further distro-specific system package automation is outside bounded scope.
